reasoning: The current world record for the men's 50m butterfly (long course) is held by Andriy Govorov, with a time of 22.27 seconds set in 2018. The 2025 World Aquatics Championships in Singapore have just been held (late July/early August 2025) and the men's 50m butterfly final was contested. Maxime Grousset won gold with a time of 22.48 seconds, indicating that the world record (22.27) was not broken during this major event. Given that 22.48 is over 0.2 seconds off the record (which is a large margin in a 50m sprint), and the biggest championships of the year has now passed, it's very unlikely the record will be broken in the rest of 2025 or early 2026. The next resolution dates are very soon (Aug 10, Sep 2, Nov 1, Jan 30). Because the major long-course meet of the year is over, long-course swimming typically enters a lull period, and the record has stood solidly since 2018, the probability of Govorov holding the record at each date is very high, decreasing slightly as the horizon extends to early 2026.
